report_name: UK Financial Regulators Put Firms on Notice Over Frontier AI Cyber Risks

identities.name: Bank of England
identities.description: One of the UK's primary financial regulators that issued a joint statement on AI-related cyber risks for financial firms.

identities.name: Financial Conduct Authority
identities.description: One of the UK's primary financial regulators that issued a joint statement on AI-related cyber risks for financial firms. Referred to as FCA.

identities.name: HM Treasury
identities.description: One of the UK's primary financial regulators that issued a joint statement on AI-related cyber risks for financial firms.

identities.name: UK Banks and Building Societies
identities.description: A class of UK financial firms regulated by the Bank of England and FCA, targeted by the joint warning on AI cyber risks.

identities.name: UK Investment Firms
identities.description: A class of UK financial firms regulated by the Bank of England and FCA, targeted by the joint warning on AI cyber risks.

identities.name: UK Insurance Companies
identities.description: A class of UK financial firms regulated by the Bank of England and FCA, targeted by the joint warning on AI cyber risks.

identities.name: UK Asset Managers
identities.description: A class of UK financial firms regulated by the Bank of England and FCA, targeted by the joint warning on AI cyber risks.

identities.name: UK Financial Market Infrastructures
identities.description: A class of UK financial firms (FMIs), such as clearing houses and payment systems, regulated by the Bank of England and FCA and targeted by the joint warning on AI cyber risks.
